title: RFC: aether-desktop
description: Research and create an RFC implementation plan for aether-desktop
result_text:
# RFC: aether-desktop Architecture & Enhancement Plan

## Summary

aether-desktop is a **mature, fully-implemented** Rust desktop application built with Dioxus 0.7.2. It provides a sophisticated multi-agent AI coding assistant interface with real-time git diff visualization, fuzzy file search, Docker integration, and comprehensive state management. The application demonstrates strong architectural patterns including dedicated thread-per-agent, pure functional state machines, and modular component design.

This RFC documents the existing implementation, validates architectural decisions, and identifies areas for enhancement including deployment automation, accessibility improvements, and performance optimization.

## Key Findings

The codebase explorer discovered that **aether-desktop already exists** with a production-ready implementation at `/Users/josh/code/aether/packages/aether-desktop/`. Key capabilities include:

- Multi-agent concurrent management with thread isolation
- Real-time git diff visualization with inline collaboration
- Local and Docker execution modes for agent isolation
- Fuzzy file search and @file mention support
- Comprehensive chat interface with streaming responses
- JSON-based settings management
- Comprehensive test coverage across all modules

**Gaps Identified:**
- No auto-update mechanism
- No installer packaging (MSI, DMG, AppImage, DEB/RPM)
- Accessibility support unverified
- Performance metrics undocumented
- No code signing or crash reporting

## Architecture Overview

**Framework:** Dioxus 0.7.2 with desktop platform support via LaunchBuilder::desktop()
**State Management:** Three-tier (GlobalSignal UI, AgentRegistry app state, AgentHandles runtime)
**Agent Runtime:** Dedicated OS threads with isolated tokio runtimes
**Components:** 18 modular UI components using RSX syntax and hooks
**File System:** Git diff engine (git2-rs), fuzzy search (nucleo), dual file watching (notify + polling)

## Key Architectural Decisions

| Decision | Rationale | Alternatives |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| Dioxus 0.7.2 | React-like ergonomics, multiplatform, existing ecosystem | Tauri (heavier), Iced (experimental), Slint (commercial) |
| Dedicated Thread per Agent | Thread isolation, clear ownership boundaries | Single thread (blocking risk), separate processes (overhead) |
| GlobalSignal Pattern | Dioxus best practice, efficient re-renders | Prop drilling (boilerplate), Context API (overhead) |
| Dual File Watching | notify for local efficiency, polling for Docker | notify only (fails in containers), polling only (slow) |
| Pure Functional State Machine | Testable without Dioxus, predictable transitions | Mixed imperative (harder test), event sourcing (overhead) |

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Documentation & Validation
- [ ] Add architecture documentation to main.rs and core modules
- [ ] Add performance logging (startup, memory, agent spawn latency)
- [ ] Create benchmark suite (target: startup < 3s, idle < 300MB)
- [ ] Test accessibility with screen readers (Narrator, VoiceOver, Orca)
- [ ] Document keyboard shortcuts and navigation patterns
- [ ] Create integration test suite for multi-agent scenarios

### Phase 2: Production Deployment
Depends on: Phase 1
- [ ] Integrate auto-update mechanism
- [ ] Configure code signing (Windows Authenticode, macOS Developer ID, Linux GPG)
- [ ] Create installer build scripts (MSI, DMG, AppImage, DEB/RPM)
- [ ] Implement crash reporting integration
- [ ] Add opt-in telemetry respecting GDPR/CCPA
- [ ] Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD for cross-platform builds

### Phase 3: Feature Enhancements
Depends on: Phase 2
- [ ] Add system tray icon with quick actions
- [ ] Implement native desktop notifications
- [ ] Add URI scheme handler (aether://task/123)
- [ ] Enhance Docker mode with image caching
- [ ] Add agent templates and quick-start wizard
- [ ] Implement multi-window support

## Testing Strategy

**Current Coverage:**
- Unit tests in all modules with tempfile isolation
- State machine tests (pure functional)
- Diff parsing, file search, file watching tests
- Settings serialization tests

**Needed:**
- Automated cross-platform testing (GitHub Actions)
- Manual QA for installers on fresh systems
- Accessibility testing with screen readers
- Performance benchmarking (startup, memory, concurrent agents)
- Integration testing for full agent lifecycles

## Risks and Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Dioxus accessibility issues | Test with screen readers; add ARIA labels |
| Binary size bloat | Profile with cargo-bloat; optimize dependencies |
| Docker mode performance | Cache images; copy-on-write layers |
| Update mechanism complexity | Use proven updater; test rollback |
| Code signing certificates | Document rotation; automated signing in CI |
| Cross-platform build complexity | GitHub Actions matrix builds |
| Memory leaks | Heap profiling; agent lifecycle limits |

## Open Questions

- Should aether-desktop require backend daemon or run agents inline?
- How to handle concurrent TUI/Desktop usage?
- Auto-update frequency and user control?
- Telemetry granularity vs privacy concerns?
- Docker image strategy (pre-built vs build-on-demand)?
- Multi-window isolation approach?
- System tray behavior (minimize vs taskbar)?
- Accessibility certification for enterprise?

## References

- [Dioxus 0.7.2 Docs](https://dioxuslabs.com/learn/0.4/)
- [2025 Rust GUI Survey](https://www.boringcactus.com/2025/04/13/2025-survey-of-rust-gui-libraries.html)
- [Are We GUI Yet?](https://areweguiyet.com/)
- [agent-client-protocol 0.5.0](https://crates.io/crates/agent-client-protocol)
